claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child I'm responsible for. I'm inquiring about my son Alex - he's 18 years old and lives at home with me. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Since Alex is 18 years old, the key factor is whether he's in approved education or training. Child Benefit can continue for children under 20 if they stay in approved education or training.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training (such as A-levels, college courses, or an approved training program)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is! Alex is studying for his A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you! Since Alex is 18 and studying for his A-levels, which is approved education, you would normally be eligible for Child Benefit.

However, I need to check a few more things to make sure:

Does Alex work for 24 hours or more per week, or does he receive any benefits in his own right (such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't work that many hours - he's focused on his studies. And he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name either.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! And just to confirm a few final details:

Do you live in the UK, and does Alex live with you at home?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - I live in the UK and Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me, let me provide you with the outcome:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Since Alex is 18, studying for his A-levels (which is approved education), doesn't work 24+ hours per week, doesn't receive benefits in his own right, and lives at home with you in the UK, you meet all the eligibility criteria.

**Important note:** Please be aware that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. Even if this applies, you can still claim Child Benefit - you would just need to pay back some or all of it through the tax system.

If you haven't already claimed Child Benefit for Alex, you can make a claim. If his circumstances change (such as finishing his A-levels, starting work for 24+ hours per week, or claiming benefits in his own right), you'll need to inform HMRC as this could affect your eligibility.

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and studying for his A-levels, which counts as approved education. Under Child Benefit rules, you normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Alex does not work 24 hours or more per week and does not receive benefits in his own right (such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit), which would disqualify him. You live in the UK and Alex lives at home with you, meaning you are responsible for him. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 18-year-old son Alex. Alex is in approved education (studying A-levels), does not work 24+ hours per week, does not receive benefits in his own right, and lives at home with the claimant in the UK. All eligibility criteria are met.
